title: AI That Composes and Creates Sound

modules.title: How Machines Learn to Hear
modules.sub: Exploring the foundations of audio perception in AI systems
modules.description: Learners examine how AI systems process and interpret sound, including the concepts of waveforms, spectrograms, and feature extraction. They build foundational literacy in audio AI by tracing how raw sound data becomes something a machine can analyze and act on.

modules.title: From MIDI to Neural Networks
modules.sub: Tracing music technology's evolution into deep learning territory
modules.description: Learners follow the historical arc from early digital music formats like MIDI to the neural network architectures powering today's generative audio models. They evaluate key milestones to understand why modern AI music tools work the way they do and what design choices shaped them.

modules.title: Text-to-Music Tools Explored
modules.sub: Hands-on testing of AI platforms that generate music from prompts
modules.description: Learners experiment with leading text-to-music platforms, generating original compositions from natural language prompts and comparing outputs across tools. They assess output quality, creative control, and practical fit for professional use cases in media and entertainment.

modules.title: AI Voice Cloning and Synthesis
modules.sub: Understanding how AI replicates and constructs human voices
modules.description: Learners explore the technology behind voice cloning and text-to-speech synthesis, examining how models are trained on vocal samples to reproduce human-like speech. They weigh the professional applications of these tools against the ethical risks of misuse and unauthorized replication.

modules.title: Copyright in the Age of AI Audio
modules.sub: Navigating ownership, licensing, and legal grey zones in AI music
modules.description: Learners analyze current copyright frameworks and how they apply — or fail to apply — to AI-generated music and cloned voices. They work through real-world scenarios to make informed decisions about rights, attribution, and risk when using or producing AI audio content professionally.

modules.title: Producers and AI: Collaboration or Competition
modules.sub: Examining AI's real impact on creative professionals in music
modules.description: Learners engage with perspectives from working producers, composers, and sound designers to assess how AI tools are changing creative workflows. They form their own position on whether AI functions as a collaborator that expands creative possibility or a competitive force displacing human talent.

modules.title: Detecting AI-Generated Audio Content
modules.sub: Developing skills to identify and verify machine-made sound
modules.description: Learners apply detection techniques and available tools to distinguish AI-generated audio from human-created recordings. They practice critical listening alongside technical analysis, building the judgment needed to verify audio authenticity in media, journalism, and production contexts.

modules.title: The Future Soundscape of AI Music
modules.sub: Anticipating where AI audio technology is headed next
modules.description: Learners synthesize what they have learned to map emerging trends in AI audio, from real-time adaptive music to fully autonomous composition systems. They develop a forward-looking perspective on how these technologies will reshape creative industries and what strategic decisions professionals should prepare for now.

synopsis: Discover how artificial intelligence is transforming music composition, sound design, and voice generation — from apps that write songs in seconds to tools that clone human voices with startling accuracy. This course equips learners to use, evaluate, and think critically about AI audio tools shaping entertainment, media, and creative industries.
rationale: As AI audio generation becomes embedded in streaming platforms, advertising, and social media, learners without foundational literacy in this area are unprepared to navigate its creative, legal, and ethical implications.
